q1.q: What's the best CRM for WhatsApp Business in 2026?
q1.a: Zyrix is purpose-built for WhatsApp-first commerce. Unlike traditional CRMs that bolt on WhatsApp as an afterthought, every Zyrix feature — pipelines, automation, AI agents, reporting — is designed around WhatsApp Business API. We integrate directly with Meta Cloud API, support template messages, broadcast lists, and 24-hour conversation windows.

q2.q: Does Zyrix support Arabic and Turkish languages natively?
q2.a: Yes. Zyrix is fully translated and adapted for Arabic (with proper RTL support) and Turkish. Our AI agents understand and respond in native Arabic and Turkish — not machine-translated quality. Customer support, documentation, and onboarding are all available in EN/AR/TR.

q3.q: Can I integrate Zyrix with my Shopify or WooCommerce store?
q3.a: Yes. Zyrix offers two-way sync with Shopify, WooCommerce, BigCommerce, and 40+ e-commerce platforms. Customer data, orders, inventory, and abandoned carts sync automatically. Setup takes 2 minutes — connect your store, authenticate, and you're live.

q4.q: Is Zyrix suitable for MENA businesses with Cash on Delivery?
q4.a: Absolutely. Zyrix has native COD lifecycle tracking — confirmation, courier pickup, delivery, cash collection, and bank reconciliation. We integrate with regional couriers including SMSA, Aramex, Naqel (KSA), MNG Kargo, Yurtiçi Kargo, J&T (Türkiye), and 25+ more.

q5.q: How does Zyrix pricing compare to HubSpot or Salesforce?
q5.a: Zyrix is up to 80% cheaper than per-seat CRMs like HubSpot or Salesforce. Our flat plan structure means no per-user fees — add unlimited team members on Business and Enterprise plans. Plus, we don't charge extra for features that should be standard (WhatsApp, AI agents, reporting).

q6.q: Is there a free trial for Zyrix CRM?
q6.a: Yes. Every paid plan starts with a 14-day free trial — no credit card required. Our Free plan is permanently free for solo founders and small teams (up to 3 users, 100 contacts). You can upgrade or cancel anytime.

q7.a: Zyrix is used by sales teams, e-commerce stores, real estate brokers, medical clinics, marketing agencies, and SaaS companies across the MENA region and Türkiye. We have dedicated solution pages and pre-built workflows for each industry.

q8.q: Does Zyrix comply with Saudi PDPL, Turkish KVKK, and GDPR?
q8.a: Yes. Zyrix is compliant with Saudi PDPL (Personal Data Protection Law), Turkish KVKK (Kişisel Verileri Koruma Kanunu), and EU GDPR. We offer data residency options in your region, full audit logs, encryption at rest and in transit, and signed Data Processing Agreements.

q9.q: Can I migrate my data from another CRM to Zyrix?
q9.a: Yes. Zyrix supports CSV import, API migration, and custom data transfer from HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, Zoho, monday.com, and most major CRMs. Our team offers free migration assistance for Business and Enterprise plans.

q10.q: What happens to my data if I cancel?
q10.a: Your data belongs to you. On cancellation, you can export everything as CSV or JSON within 90 days. After that, we permanently delete your data per our retention policy. We never sell your data, never use it to train AI models for other companies, and you can request deletion at any time.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains what information Zyrix collects when you use our CRM platform, why we collect it, and what choices you have. We take your privacy seriously and designed Zyrix to give you real control over your data.

s1.title: 1. Data we collect
s1.p1: We collect only what we need to deliver the service. Broadly, this falls into five categories:

l1.label: Account data
l1.body: Your name, email, phone number, company name, company country, and the password hash (never the password itself).

l2.label: Business data you create
l2.body: Customers, deals, activities, notes, tasks, quotes, and any content you choose to store in Zyrix.

l3.label: Payment data
l3.body: Your plan, billing cycle, currency, invoice email, and transaction history. Card numbers are never stored on our servers — they are handled entirely by our payment providers.

l4.label: Usage data
l4.body: Which pages you open, which features you use, timestamps, IP address, browser and device type. This helps us fix bugs and prioritize improvements.

l5.label: Communications
l5.body: Support tickets, email conversations, and any messages you send us.

s2.title: 2. How we use your data
s2.p1: We use your data only for specific, narrow purposes:
s2.l1: To provide the CRM service you signed up for.
s2.l2: To process payments and send invoices and receipts.
s2.l3: To respond to your support requests.
s2.l4: To send essential service emails (security alerts, password resets, payment failures, subscription changes).
s2.l5: To improve the product — in aggregate, non-identifiable form only.

s3.title: 3. Third-party providers
s3.p1: We use a small number of vetted vendors to operate Zyrix. Each of them has their own privacy policy and processes data only on our instructions:
s3.hyperpay: Payment processing for Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and the wider GCC (SAR, AED, USD).
s3.resend: Transactional email delivery (verification, password reset, receipts).
s3.google: Sign-in via Google account. We only receive your email and public profile — nothing else.
s3.gemini: AI features. When you use an AI feature, the prompt and relevant data are sent to Google Gemini. Google does not use this data to train its models.
s3.p2: We do not sell your data to anyone. We do not share your data with advertisers. We do not use your business data to train AI models.

s4.title: 4. Where your data is stored
s4.p1: Zyrix uses cloud infrastructure to operate. Your data is stored in the following regions depending on the service:
s4.l1: Application servers and database — Railway (US/EU regions).
s4.l2: Static assets — Vercel global edge network.
s4.l3: DNS and security — Cloudflare global network.
s4.l4: We may transfer data across borders for service delivery. When we do, we rely on standard contractual clauses or other legally recognized mechanisms.

s5.title: 5. Security
s5.p1: We apply strong technical and organizational measures to protect your data:
s5.l1: All traffic is encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher.
s5.l2: Passwords are hashed using bcrypt with a strong cost factor.
s5.l3: Access tokens are short-lived (15 minutes) and refresh tokens are rotated.
s5.l4: Database access is restricted to a small number of production engineers.
s5.l5: All administrative actions are logged in an immutable audit log.
s5.l6: We never store raw payment card numbers.
s5.p2: No system is ever 100% secure. If you believe your account has been compromised, email us immediately at security@zyrix.co.

s6.title: 6. Your rights
s6.p1: You have full rights over your personal data. You can exercise any of these at any time by emailing privacy@zyrix.co:

l1.label: Access
l1.body: Request a copy of all personal data we hold about you.

l2.label: Correction
l2.body: Fix any inaccurate or incomplete information.

l3.label: Deletion
l3.body: Delete your account and all associated business data. Note: invoice records must be retained for legal and tax purposes.

l4.label: Export
l4.body: Receive your business data in a structured, machine-readable format (CSV or JSON).

s7.title: 7. Data retention
s7.p1: We keep your business data for as long as your account is active. If you delete your account, we remove your business data within 30 days.
s7.p2: Invoices, payment records, and audit logs are retained for up to 10 years to comply with accounting and anti-money-laundering regulations in Türkiye and the GCC.

s8.title: 8. Cookies
s8.p1: We use only essential cookies required to keep you signed in and remember your language preference. We do not use marketing or advertising cookies on any page of crm.zyrix.co.

s9.title: 9. Children
s9.p1: Zyrix is a business product. It is not directed at anyone under 18. We do not knowingly collect data from minors.

s10.title: 10. Changes to this policy
s10.p1: We may update this policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we notify all active customers by email at least 15 days before the changes take effect. The 'Last updated' date at the top always reflects the current version.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service are the agreement between you (the customer) and Zyrix (the provider) when you use our CRM platform at crm.zyrix.co and its subdomains. By creating an account, you accept these terms.

s1.title: 1. Who we are
s1.p1: Zyrix is a software company based in Istanbul, Türkiye. Zyrix CRM is a subscription-based customer relationship management platform designed for businesses operating in Türkiye and the Arab world.

s2.title: 2. Eligibility
s2.l1: Be at least 18 years old and legally able to enter a binding contract.
s2.l2: Represent a legitimate business entity or be acting on behalf of one.
s2.l3: Provide accurate registration information and keep it up to date.
s2.l4: Comply with all laws that apply to your business and your customers.

s3.title: 3. Your account
s3.p1: You are responsible for everything that happens under your account:

l1.label: Credentials
l1.body: Keep your password confidential. Do not share it with anyone.

l2.label: Authorized users
l2.body: You may invite team members; the number of seats depends on your plan.

l3.label: Accuracy
l3.body: Do not sign up with false identity or false business information.

l4.label: Legal activity
l4.body: You may not use Zyrix for any illegal purpose, including fraud, money laundering, or violating sanctions.

s4.title: 4. Subscription, billing, and refunds
s4.p1: Paid plans auto-renew at the end of each billing cycle (monthly or yearly) using the payment method on file. You can cancel at any time from your account — cancellation takes effect at the end of the current paid period and stops future charges.
s4.p2: We do not offer pro-rated refunds for partial months or unused portions. If a charge was clearly erroneous or duplicated, contact us at billing@zyrix.co within 30 days and we will investigate in good faith.
s4.p3: Prices may change; we will give at least 30 days' notice before raising prices on your current plan.

s5.title: 5. Acceptable use
s5.l1: Send spam, phishing, or unsolicited bulk communications through Zyrix or its integrations (including WhatsApp).
s5.l2: Upload malware, viruses, or content designed to damage any system.
s5.l3: Attempt to reverse-engineer, resell, or sublicense the platform without our written permission.
s5.l4: Use the service to store or process data you do not have the right to process.
s5.l5: Harass or threaten other users, our staff, or third parties through the platform.
s5.l6: Violate the terms of any integrated third party (for example, WhatsApp Business Policy).

s6.title: 6. Your data
s6.p1: You own the data you enter into Zyrix. We do not claim any ownership over your customer records, deals, notes, or any business data. You grant us the minimal license required to host, display, and process your data solely to provide the service.
s6.p2: You can export or delete your data at any time. When you delete your account, we remove your business data within 30 days (see our Privacy Policy for details on what we are legally required to retain).

s7.title: 7. Availability and uptime
s7.p1: We aim for 99.9% monthly uptime. Scheduled maintenance is announced in advance whenever possible. We are not liable for downtime caused by events outside our reasonable control, including upstream provider outages (Railway, Vercel, Cloudflare, payment gateways).

s8.title: 8. Disclaimer and limitation of liability
s8.p1: Zyrix is provided 'as is' without any warranty of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose. Our total liability for any claim arising from your use of the service is limited to the amount you paid us in the 12 months before the claim arose. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages including lost profits or lost business opportunities.

s9.title: 9. Termination
s9.p1: We may suspend or terminate your account if you violate these terms, abuse the service, or fail to pay. In most cases we will notify you first and give you a chance to fix the issue. For severe violations (fraud, illegal activity, security attacks) we may terminate immediately.

s10.title: 10. Governing law and contact
s10.p1: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Türkiye. Any dispute arising from these terms will be handled by the courts of Istanbul. Contact us for legal matters at:

About.title: Built in Istanbul, for the Arab world and Türkiye
About.subtitle: Zyrix is a CRM designed from day one for the way businesses actually operate in Türkiye, the Gulf, Egypt, and Iraq — not a translation of a Silicon Valley product.

story.heading: We got tired of forcing US tools to speak Arabic
story.p1: Our founder spent years running e-commerce and marketing operations across Türkiye, Iraq, and Saudi Arabia — managing campaigns in Arabic, invoices in Turkish lira, and customer support across three timezones.
story.p2: Every CRM he tried was built for the US market and bolted on other languages as an afterthought. Arabic was right-to-left but half-translated. Turkish KDV was 'almost' supported. WhatsApp — the actual channel his customers used — needed a $99/month plugin.
story.p3: Zyrix exists to fix that. A CRM where Arabic, Turkish, and English are equal citizens. Where WhatsApp is in the core, not a plugin. Where KDV and VAT just work. Built in Istanbul by a team that lives in the same time zone as the customer you're trying to close.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.body: Give businesses in Türkiye and the Arab world the same quality of tooling that Silicon Valley keeps for itself — in their own language, at a fair price, with support they can actually reach.

vision.title: Our vision
vision.body: To become the default CRM for every business operating between Istanbul and Riyadh. Not by copying legacy enterprise tools — by being genuinely better at the things that matter here: Arabic, WhatsApp, local payments, and regional compliance.

values.heading: The principles we won't compromise on
values.subtitle: These aren't posters on a wall — they drive what we build and what we refuse to build.

customerFirst.title: Customer-first, every time
customerFirst.body: We don't chase vanity metrics. If a feature doesn't make a customer's day easier, we don't build it.

region.title: Regional, not global-minus
region.body: We're not a watered-down version of a US product. We're built for this region, in this region.

ship.title: Ship, then learn
ship.body: We deploy every week. We'd rather ship something imperfect and improve it than wait six months to be wrong.

transparent.title: Transparent by default
transparent.body: Honest roadmaps, honest pricing, honest about what works and what doesn't. Trust compounds.

team.heading: A small team, close to the customer
team.subtitle: We're intentionally lean. Everyone on the team talks to customers every week.
team.founderBio: Built Zyrix from scratch after years running marketing and e-commerce operations across Türkiye, Iraq, and Saudi Arabia. MBA, 10+ years in digital marketing, media buying, and product — with a deep belief that the Arab world and Türkiye deserve first-class software built for them, not translated to them.

pain.point3: Mobile apps are stripped-down toys. Reps in the field can't update deals, can't access notes, can't close from their phone.

workflow.title: From lead to closed-won in 5 steps
workflow.subtitle: How a typical deal flows through Zyrix in real Arab and Turkish sales contexts.

step1.title: Lead arrives via WhatsApp
step1.desc: Customer sends message to your business number. Zyrix auto-creates a contact and lead, tagged with source = WhatsApp.

step2.title: Auto-assigned to right rep
step2.desc: Round-robin or by region/language/product. Rep gets a push notification. Contact details + conversation history already in the deal.

step3.title: Rep qualifies + sends quote
step3.desc: Rep replies in WhatsApp from inside Zyrix. Sends product catalog. Generates a quote PDF in 30 seconds.

step4.title: Follow-ups happen automatically
step4.desc: No reply in 2 days? Zyrix sends a polite WhatsApp follow-up. Still no reply in 5 days? Manager gets a nudge. Deal never falls through.

step5.title: Close + handover to fulfillment
step5.desc: Customer agrees. Rep clicks 'Won'. Deal moves to fulfillment pipeline. Customer gets confirmation message. Internal Slack/Teams notification fires. Done.

pain.title: Western e-commerce CRMs don't understand our market.
pain.subtitle: Selling in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Iraq, or Türkiye? You face problems Shopify wasn't built for:
pain.point1: 70% of orders are COD. Most CRMs have no concept of COD lifecycle — confirmed → shipped → collected → reconciled.
pain.point2: Arabic and Turkish customers expect WhatsApp replies in minutes, not 24 hours via support ticket. Bots that don't speak Arabic well lose trust fast.
pain.point3: Shipping in MENA means dozens of local couriers (SMSA, Aramex Saudi, Naqel, MNG, Yurtiçi…). Western CRMs only know UPS and FedEx.

workflow.title: How a WhatsApp order flows through Zyrix
workflow.subtitle: A real customer journey for an Arab consumer buying skincare from a Saudi Shopify store.

step1.title: Customer messages on WhatsApp
step1.desc: Customer sees Instagram ad, taps 'Message us'. WhatsApp opens. Zyrix auto-greets in Arabic and shows the product catalog.

step2.title: Browses + adds to cart
step2.desc: Customer browses catalog inside WhatsApp. Adds 3 items. Zyrix calculates total in SAR including shipping and VAT. Cart held for 60 minutes.

step3.title: Checks out — selects COD
step3.desc: Customer enters address. Selects Cash on Delivery. Order is confirmed. Zyrix creates the order in Shopify, books the courier (SMSA), generates the shipping label.

step4.title: Auto-confirmation + tracking
step4.desc: Customer gets WhatsApp confirmation with order number and SMSA tracking URL. Zyrix monitors the SMSA API. Updates customer when picked up, when out for delivery.

step5.title: Delivery + cash reconciliation
step5.desc: Courier delivers, collects cash. SMSA syncs the status to Zyrix. Cash is reconciled to the merchant's bank deposit on day 7. Order marked complete. Customer added to retargeting segment.

pain.title: Real estate is local. Your CRM should be too.
pain.subtitle: Selling property in Saudi Arabia or Türkiye is different from selling in California. Three pain points:
pain.point1: Buyers contact 5-10 brokers at once via WhatsApp. The broker who replies first wins. Reply in 15 minutes or lose the lead.
pain.point2: Property contracts have regional specifics — KSA's Tabu system, Türkiye's iskan certificates, Egypt's contract notarization. Generic CRMs ignore them.
pain.point3: Brokers in the field need everything mobile. Western desktop-first CRMs are useless when you're walking a property with a buyer.

workflow.title: How a property deal flows through Zyrix
workflow.subtitle: A real journey for a Saudi family buying a villa in north Riyadh.

step1.title: Lead from Sahibinden / Property Finder
step1.desc: Buyer contacts via property listing. Zyrix creates the lead, attaches the property, notes their budget and preferred districts.

step2.title: Broker replies within 5 minutes
step2.desc: Auto-assigned broker gets a push notification. Replies on WhatsApp with 3 similar properties + 360° tour links. All from inside Zyrix.

step3.title: Viewings booked + attended
step3.desc: Family books 3 viewings via the broker's calendar link. Reminders sent. Broker logs viewing notes after each — what they liked, dislikes, body language.

step4.title: Offer + negotiation
step4.desc: Family sends offer. Owner counter-offers. Broker tracks the negotiation thread. Documents (income proof, ID copies) collected via secure upload. All inside the deal.

step5.title: Contract + handover
step5.desc: Final terms agreed. Tabu transfer paperwork generated from template. E-signature collected. Deposit paid. Tabu registration scheduled. Family hands over keys 4 weeks later. Broker earns commission, marked in pipeline.

pain.title: Patient experience is your competitive edge.
pain.subtitle: Healthcare is local, personal, and increasingly competitive. Three challenges every clinic faces:

Playbook.title: The WhatsApp Sales Playbook for MENA + Türkiye
Playbook.subtitle: Six chapters of hard-won lessons from 500+ businesses selling on WhatsApp across Saudi Arabia, the Gulf, Türkiye, and Egypt. Free. Tactical. Battle-tested.

ch1.title: The first reply wins (or loses) the deal
ch1.intro: On WhatsApp, the customer messages 5-10 businesses at once. Whoever replies first earns the right to negotiate. The math is brutal: reply within 5 minutes and you have a 70% chance of winning the conversation; reply after 30 minutes and you're at 8%.

rule1.title: Rule 1: Sub-5-minute first reply
rule1.body: Build your team's first-reply SLA at under 5 minutes during business hours. This is non-negotiable. Hire enough agents, automate the initial qualifier, or both — but never let the customer wait.

example1.title: Example: Saudi e-commerce store, skincare
example1.body: Customer messages 'Hi, I'm interested in your serum.' Bad reply (after 47 minutes): 'Hi! Yes, here is the product link.' Good reply (after 90 seconds): 'Hi Mariam, welcome to Levana. The vitamin C serum is our best-seller — would you like to see results from someone with similar skin? Also, are you in Riyadh? We deliver same-day there.'

rule2.title: Rule 2: Personalize the first message
rule2.body: Use their name (WhatsApp shows it). Reference what they asked about. Mention their region if known. Generic 'Welcome to our store' replies feel like spam — and customers can tell.

warning1.title: Don't use auto-responders that pretend to be human
warning1.body: Customers know when they're talking to a bot. If you're using automation for the first reply, be honest: 'A team member will reply in under 5 minutes — meanwhile, here's our catalog.' Honesty builds trust. Pretending erodes it.

ch2.title: Match the response cadence to the channel
ch2.intro: WhatsApp isn't email. The expected response time is 5-15 minutes during business hours, not 24-48 hours. Customers will leave you for a competitor who replies faster.

rule1.title: Set explicit business hours — and respect them
rule1.body: If you're closed at 11pm, say so in your WhatsApp Business profile and your auto-reply. Customers respect honest boundaries. They lose patience with silent ghosting.

example1.title: Example: Setting expectations
example1.body: Off-hours auto-reply: 'Thanks for reaching out! Our team is offline (10pm-9am UAE time). We'll reply first thing tomorrow morning. For urgent orders, browse our catalog: [link]. Or we'll get back to you at 9am sharp.'

rule2.title: Never let a conversation go cold mid-thread
rule2.body: If you're researching something for the customer (checking stock, getting approval), say so explicitly: 'Let me check this with our warehouse, I'll have an answer in 10 minutes.' Then deliver. Silent gaps kill deals.

ch3.title: Templates are leverage, not laziness
ch3.intro: Hand-typing every reply doesn't scale. Templates do — but only if they're written well. Bad templates feel robotic. Good templates feel like a smart human who anticipated the conversation.

rule1.title: Build templates for the top 20 questions
rule1.body: Audit your team's last 500 customer messages. Identify the 20 most common questions ('Do you ship to Kuwait?', 'How do I pay?', 'When does it arrive?'). Write a great template for each. Train your team to start from the template and personalize.

example1.title: Template: Shipping question
example1.body: 'Great question! We ship to all GCC countries. Delivery times: KSA 1-2 days, UAE 2-3 days, Kuwait/Qatar 3-4 days, Bahrain/Oman 4-5 days. All shipping is free for orders over 200 SAR. Want me to share tracking once your order ships?'

example2.title: Template: Payment options
example2.body: 'We accept: 💳 Mada/Visa/Mastercard online, 💵 Cash on Delivery (KSA only), 🏦 Bank transfer (immediate). For your first order, COD is most popular — only pay when you receive the package and inspect it. Want to try that?'

warning1.title: Personalize before sending
warning1.body: Replace [Name] with the actual name. Adjust tone to match the conversation. Add a relevant question at the end. Templates are starting points, not final messages. Customers can smell a copy-paste.

ch4.title: Build a team workflow that scales
ch4.intro: One person can handle 50 conversations a day. Past that, you need structure: who replies first, when do specialists step in, what gets escalated to a manager, and how is the workload distributed.

rule1.title: Triage with the 3-tier model
rule1.body: Tier 1: Generalist agent handles all first replies and basic questions. Tier 2: Product specialist handles technical questions, custom requests. Tier 3: Manager handles complaints, refunds, enterprise deals. Clear handoffs with notes — no customer should re-explain themselves.

rule2.title: Round-robin or geographic routing
rule2.body: Round-robin works for small teams. For larger teams, route by language (Arabic-speaker to Arab agent), region (Saudi customer to KSA agent who knows local context), or product line. Customers get faster, more relevant replies.

example1.title: Example: Handoff with context
example1.body: Bad handoff: 'Hi, I'm taking over from Ahmed.' (Customer thinks: who is Ahmed? what did we discuss?) Good handoff: 'Hi Layla, this is Sarah. I see Ahmed was helping you choose between the 30ml and 50ml serum. The 30ml is our most popular for first-time customers — want to start with that and upgrade if you love it?'

ch5.title: Automate the boring. Keep the human.
ch5.intro: Automation is your friend for the predictable parts of the conversation: order confirmations, shipping updates, follow-ups. It's your enemy for the moments that matter: complaints, custom requests, complex questions.

rule1.title: Automate post-purchase, not pre-purchase
rule1.body: After a customer buys: order confirmation, shipping notifications, delivery reminders, post-delivery follow-up — all great for automation. Before they buy: keep humans in the loop. The pre-purchase conversation is where deals are won.

example1.title: Example: 4-step post-purchase flow
example1.body: 1. Order confirmation (immediate): 'Thanks for your order! Your tracking number is [X]. Estimated delivery: Wednesday.' 2. Shipping update (when shipped): 'Your order is on the way! Track here: [link].' 3. Delivery confirmation (when delivered): 'Your order arrived. Hope you love it! Need anything?' 4. Follow-up (3 days later): 'How's the product working out? We'd love your feedback.'

warning1.title: Don't automate apologies
warning1.body: Bot-generated 'We're sorry to hear that' for a complaint is worse than no reply at all. Customers feel disrespected. For complaints, refunds, and complex issues — always have a real human respond. Zyrix's auto-routing flags complaints for immediate human attention.

ch6.title: Advanced tactics for high-volume operators
ch6.intro: If you're past 1,000 conversations a month, you need techniques that smaller operators don't. Catalogs in WhatsApp, broadcast lists, integrations with your CRM — these are force multipliers when used right.

rule1.title: Use the catalog as your storefront
rule1.body: WhatsApp Catalog (in WhatsApp Business) lets you display products inline. Customers browse without leaving the chat. For e-commerce: this dramatically reduces friction vs. sending external links. Set up your catalog properly and watch conversion rates climb.

rule2.title: Broadcast lists, not group chats
rule2.body: Broadcast lists send to 256+ contacts at once but each recipient sees a private message. Group chats are for community. Don't confuse them. For sales updates, abandoned cart reminders, restock alerts — broadcast lists. For VIP communities and events — groups.

example1.title: Example: Broadcast list use cases
example1.body: Restock alert: 'Hi! The Hyaluronic Serum is back in stock. We sold out 3 days early last time. Tap to order before it's gone again.' Abandoned cart: 'Hi Sarah, you left these in your cart yesterday: [products]. Order in the next 4 hours and shipping is on us.' Customer appreciation: 'Hi Mohammed, you've been with us 6 months. Here's 20% off your next order: [code]. Thank you for being a Levana customer.'
